inputs.label: Body Weight
inputs.businessContext: User's body weight directly influences energy expenditure; heavier individuals burn more calories.

inputs.label: Duration
inputs.businessContext: Time spent actively snowboarding; longer duration increases total calorie burn.

inputs.label: Intensity Level
inputs.businessContext: Perceived exertion from 1 (very light, like easy cruising) to 10 (extreme, like aggressive carving or backcountry); used to derive metabolic equivalent (MET).

inputs.label: Altitude
inputs.businessContext: Above sea level altitude of the snowboarding location; higher altitude increases metabolic rate due to reduced oxygen availability.

inputs.label: Correction Factor
inputs.businessContext: Personal adjustment factor to account for individual metabolic variations (e.g., fitness level, genetics); typical range 0.8–1.2.
